title: Error User-selected graph driver "overlay" overwritten by graph driver "btrfs" from database
body:
### Issue Description

My user config has the btrfs driver.
The podman info shows me I use the btrfs driver.  
The database uses the btrfs driver.
My pods are running.

Still I see that error message.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

1. Migrate the DB in v5.8.2 via the terminal command
2. Reboot
3. Update to v5.8.3
4. Reboot
5. Update to v6.0.0
6. See that my containers.conf and storage.conf got renamed to *.pacsave because they don't exist anymore in /etc/containers
7. Move them to ~/.config/containers/ and diff them to match the files in "/usr/share/containers/" except my diffs
8. My ~/.config/containers/storage.conf contains 'driver = "btrfs"'
9. Reboot
10. Seeing the error message about btrfs everywhere

### Describe the results you received

Error message

> ERRO[0000] User-selected graph driver "overlay" overwritten by graph driver "btrfs" from database - delete libpod local files ("/home/myhostname/.local/share/containers/storage") to resolve.  May prevent use of images created by other tools 

### Describe the results you expected

No error message

### podman info output

comments.body:
I can just guess since you did not provide all config files on the system but I doubt this is a bug, rather it works as design with the new config file loading of drop ins https://blog.podman.io/2026/06/podman-6-configuration-file-changes/

I assume arch follows the fedora config file layout and distro default are shipped as drop in /usr/share/containers/00-vendor.conf which sets overlay.

So you need to put your own config in /etc/containers/storage.conf.d/01-... or ~/.config/containers//storage.conf.d/01-... to get loader after the vendor/distro defaults

Thank you very much.
Moving my two config files from
- ~/.config/containers/containers.conf -> ~/.config/containers/containers.conf.d/99-containers.conf
- ~/.config/containers/storage.conf -> ~/.config/containers/storage.conf.d/99-storage.conf

worked perfectly!

Confirming Luap99's diagnosis: on CachyOS the cause is /usr/share/containers/storage.conf.d/00-storage-arch.conf shipping driver = "overlay", which overrides the user's /etc/containers/storage.conf. The error itself is by design.

Workaround that works on this host (rootless, UID 1000):

  mkdir -p ~/.config/containers/storage.conf.d
  cat > ~/.config/containers/storage.conf.d/99-btrfs.conf <<EOF
  [storage]
  driver = "btrfs"
  EOF

This user's drop-in sorts after 00-storage-arch.conf, so the btrfs value wins. After this, podman info shows graphDriverName: btrfs cleanly with no ERRO. No data loss, no rebuild, no vendor package changes.
